Method–provenance block (reproducible summary)

• Property: condensed Fukui‐function indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰) and dual descriptor for aniline; headline number reported here is the largest electrophilic Fukui index, f⁺ = 2.2975867 on atom 3 (C).

• Level of theory: DFT, tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting (PySCF).

• Geometry provenance: fixed input geometry from file /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz; single-point calculations for N, N + 1 (cation, doublet) and N – 1 (anion, doublet) electron counts — no additional geometry optimisation (DFT//input-xyz).

• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit or explicit solvation).

• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (0, 1) for the N-electron reference; cation (+1, 2) and anion (−1, 2) states for Fukui finite differences.

• Software / version: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill calling PySCF 2.x (DFT backend) with Mulliken charge partitioning.

• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true.
  Warnings returned by the engine (verbatim):
    – sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
    – common header present

• Reproducibility: command used (from cli_invocation):
      /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py \
          fukui --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 \
          /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz \
          --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpvaxa8gs9/live.json
  Live engine log was streamed to the standard *.out* file generated by the chemkit runner (path was provided at launch).  
  Result JSON saved at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpvaxa8gs9/live.json.

Key numerical results (Mulliken‐based condensed Fukui indices, units = e):
  Atom 3 (C):  f⁺ = 2.2975867   (largest electrophilic site)
  Atom 1 (N):  f⁻ = 0.20452359  (most nucleophilic site)
  f⁰ and dual‐descriptor values for all 14 atoms are available in the result JSON.

The headline value above (2.2975867) is provided exactly as returned by the chemkit engine without rounding.
